sectionOrderRules: Hero must state what kinds of textile products the company manufactures and include a primary CTA.
Product summary should show concrete product categories or manufacturing areas, not vague claims.
Production summary should appear before the final CTA and briefly explain materials, methods or customization possibilities.
Trust proof must rely on real references, visible company details or documented experience, never invented certifications or quantities.
Sections should avoid repeating the same claims about quality or flexibility.
Products intro must connect the company's manufacturing focus to the listed product areas.
Product list items must be specific textile product categories, uses or customer segments, not generic placeholders.
If pricing overview is shown, it should present honest starting points, examples or quotation logic rather than misleading fixed promises.
Production intro must explain how the company works with manufacturing, customization or series production in concrete terms.
Process steps should follow a logical sequence such as inquiry, specification, production and delivery.
Materials and capabilities must describe actual fabrics, techniques, volumes or customization options only when supported by input data.
Trust proof, if present, should reinforce reliability without repeating the same evidence already shown in adjacent sections.
About story must describe the business in specific, factual terms and may include history, niche or working philosophy.
Team should only be rendered when actual people or roles are available.
Trust proof should include visible company information such as organisation number, references or practical experience when available.
Contact info must include real contact paths available in the source data.
Contact form should support quote or inquiry intent relevant to textile manufacturing.
Map and opening hours should only render when valid location or hours data exists.
